request.messages: [redacted: candidate evidence omitted from raw judge artifact]

message.reasoning_content:
We are asked to validate a batch of extracted ASHRAE Guideline 36 items. For each candidate, determine if it is valid (useful, grounded operational knowledge). Output JSON with verdicts.

Let's examine each candidate:

1. cand_e826fcdf839c290a: Title "Supply Fan Start/Stop", section 5.17.1.1, summary about fan runs during modes, minimum runtime. Evidence quote provided. Seems operational sequence, grounded. Accept? Yes.

2. cand_0e469db223307945: Title "Totalize Airflow from VAV Boxes", section 5.17.1.1, summary about totalizing airflow. Evidence quote. Accept? Yes.

3. cand_d93f70cdc8f291e9: Title "Static Pressure Setpoint Reset using T&R Logic", section 5.17.1.2, summary about reset using T&R logic. Accept? Yes.

4. cand_789839786224ff26: Title "Supply Air Temperature Control Loop Enable/Disable", section 5.17.2.1, summary about enable/disable based on fan status. Accept? Yes.

5. cand_790c75c342e49bac: Title "Supply Air Temperature Setpoint Reset during Occupied Mode", section 5.17.2.2, summary about SAT reset using T&R logic. Accept? Yes.

6. cand_64acad48f22d4c4b: Title "Supply Air Temperature Setpoint during Warmup and Setback Modes", section 5.17.2.2, summary about setpoint = Max_HtgSAT. Accept? Yes.

7. cand_dcd881e52b52cf67: Title "Supply Air Temperature Control via PID Loop", section 5.17.2.3, summary about PID loop modulating heating coil. Accept? Yes.

8. cand_c9508e7a8ef57d05: Title "Maintenance Interval Alarm", section 5.17.3.1, summary about alarm for >1500 hours, reset on acknowledge. Accept? Yes, it's operational diagnostic rule.

9. cand_85bdfd960e21c536: Title "Fan Status Mismatch Alarm", section 5.17.3.2, summary about alarm for status mismatch. Accept? Yes.

10. cand_d5a17eaf3ed83609: Title "Filter Pressure Drop Alarm", section 5.17.3.3, summary about alarm with formula. Evidence quote truncated. But summary includes formula: DPx = DP100 * (x)^1.4. Acceptable.

11. cand_c72582d0284f6124: Title "AFDD: Duct Static Pressure Too Low with Fan at Full Speed (FC#1)", section 5.17.4.5, summary about fault condition. Accept? Yes, AFDD operational rule.

12. cand_c4eacdd12650cb1c: Title "AFDD: SAT Too Low in Full Heating (FC#2)", section 5.17.4.5, similar. Accept.

13. cand_e535235e623b218a: Title "AFDD: Temperature Rise Across Inactive Heating Coil (FC#3)", section 5.17.4.5, similar. Accept.

14. cand_875baf6d0d70695e: Title "AFDD: Evaluation Suspension Conditions", section 5.17.4.7, summary about suspension conditions. Accept? Yes, operational.

15. cand_5b57e82b35bffc89: Title "AFDD: Alarm Delay for Fault Reporting", section 5.17.4.8, summary about AlarmDelay. Accept.

16. cand_cda65871d75bfa05: Title "AFDD: Test Mode for Instant Testing", section 5.17.4.9, summary about test mode. Accept.

17. cand_c85ac75b8eef3d83: Title "AFDD: Fault Condition Alarm Level and Content", section 5.17.4.10, summary about level 3 alarm. Accept.

All items appear to be valid operational sequences and fault diagnostic rules from ASHRAE Guideline 36. No rejections needed. Each has a candidate_id, title, section, summary, evidence quote. They are all grounded. No duplicates or unsupported summaries. So all are valid.

Now output JSON with verdicts: for each, is_valid_guideline36_knowledge = true, reason = "Operational sequence/fault diagnostic rule from Guideline 36", category_if_not = null.

Construct JSON object.
